title: FIX-467: Merge Conflict Resolution in helix-cli tickets/index.ts
description: This tour walks through the conflict resolution run for FIX-467 in the helix-cli repository. A collateral merge conflict arose in src/tickets/index.ts when HLX-342 (CLI ticket subcommand improvements: help text, JSON output, update-description subcommand) landed alongside a staging change. The conflict was already resolved before this run — zero conflict markers remained. The agent verified the resolution by scanning for markers, confirmed all 10 subcommands were preserved, and validated the file compiles cleanly via tsc --noEmit. No source code changes were required; this tour documents the verification artifacts produced.

steps.description: The scout phase mapped the problem space. merge-conflicts.json listed src/tickets/index.ts as the sole conflicted file — 4 commits from the HLX-342 ticket branch (CLI improvements) versus 1 staging commit. The scout confirmed that no conflict markers remained in the file and that the CLI has no involvement in library credential routing, making this a purely collateral conflict. It also identified the key quality gate: tsc --noEmit (typecheck).
steps.title: Scout: Identify the Conflicted File

steps.description: The diagnosis phase confirmed the root cause and current state. A grep scan of src/tickets/index.ts returned zero matches for <<<<<<<, =======, and >>>>>>>. The file was 149 lines with all 10 subcommands intact (list, latest, get, create, update-description, rerun, continue, artifacts, artifact, bundle). The single success criterion — no conflict markers — was already met, so no code changes were needed.
steps.title: Diagnosis: Conflict Already Resolved

steps.description: The APL (Articulate, Plan, Log) artifact for diagnosis captures the formal evidence chain. The answer to 'Do any conflict markers remain?' is No, backed by three evidence items: the grep scan result, the 149-line file with all 10 subcommands present, and the scout's reference-map confirming no markers. This establishes the baseline that drives the no-code-changes decision in the implementation plan.
steps.title: Diagnosis APL: Evidence of Clean File

steps.description: Because the conflict was already resolved, the implementation plan defined a verify-only workflow with four steps: (1) npm install, (2) grep scan to confirm zero markers, (3) write a .env file for the CLI environment, (4) run npm run typecheck. The plan explicitly states 'No code changes' as principle #1 and 'Quality gates only' as principle #2. This prevents accidental modification of a correctly resolved file while still providing a compilation proof.
steps.title: Implementation Plan: Verify-Only Approach

steps.description: The APL for implementation planning answers two key questions. First, is the conflict resolved? Yes — grep returned zero marker matches, the file has 149 lines with all 10 subcommands including update-description. Second, does the CLI have library credential routing logic? No — the CLI is a command-line tool for the Helix API only, with no GitHub repo provisioning or auth mode logic. This second question was critical to confirm the conflict was truly collateral and required no functional fix.
steps.title: Implementation Plan APL: No Library Involvement

steps.description: The implementation agent executed the four planned steps. npm install succeeded. The .env was written. The grep scan returned zero conflict marker matches. npm run typecheck (tsc --noEmit) exited with code 0 and no type errors — proving the resolved merge compiles correctly. No files were modified. The single check CHK-01 (CLI TypeScript type checking) passed, confirming the resolved src/tickets/index.ts is syntactically and type-safely correct.
steps.title: Implementation Actual: Steps Executed and Outcomes

steps.description: The implementation APL captures two final evidence items: zero conflict markers in tickets/index.ts (grep: no matches), and CLI typecheck passing (tsc --noEmit exits 0). Together these close the loop — the merge resolution is both structurally complete (no markers) and semantically valid (no type errors). The statement 'No library involvement in CLI' reaffirms the collateral nature of the conflict.
steps.title: Implementation APL: Typecheck Passes

steps.description: An independent code review confirmed all findings. The reviewer verified: zero conflict markers (grep confirmed), all 10 subcommand imports at lines 4-13 present, all 10 switch cases at lines 43-148 present with correct help text and handler delegation, ticket reference resolution preserved where applicable. No requirements gaps, no correctness issues, no regression risks, and no new test gaps were found. CHK-01 remains valid and unaffected.
steps.title: Code Review: Independent Verification

steps.description: The final APL artifact closes the review. Both review questions are answered with evidence: merge conflict markers are resolved (grep returned no matches for any of the three marker patterns), and all CLI subcommands are preserved (list, latest, get, create, update-description, rerun, continue, artifacts, artifact, bundle — imports at lines 4-13, switch cases at lines 43-148). The statement confirms the collateral conflict is resolved, typecheck passes, and no code changes were needed. No follow-up actions remain.
steps.title: Code Review APL: All 10 Subcommands Confirmed
